nodes.label: AI as unbiased advice source
nodes.label_negative: AI as biased or incomplete advice source
nodes.grounding_span: I just use the suggestions to get some unbiased advice that I may not have considered

nodes.label: AI as efficiency tool for research
nodes.label_negative: AI as time-consuming or unreliable research tool
nodes.grounding_span: AI can pull the answer for me in seconds, while a Google search would require going through many sites

nodes.label: AI accuracy and truthfulness
nodes.grounding_span: AI model provides me with information that is false but presents it as true

nodes.label: AI as support for new role learning
nodes.label_negative: AI as crutch hindering independent skill development
nodes.grounding_span: I ask AI questions about new terminology

nodes.label: AI use in personal student assessment
nodes.grounding_span: Asking for an AI model to create all comments about one of my students seems impersonal

nodes.label: AI as conversational partner for reflection
nodes.grounding_span: When I'm seeking advice or reflecting on my teaching pedagogy, I'm more likely to have a back-and-forth conversation

nodes.label: Professional competence and confidence
nodes.grounding_span: I will feel more confident in my day-to-day interactions

nodes.label: Personal connection with students
nodes.grounding_span: I like to come up with my own feedback since I know the students best

nodes.label: Efficiency and time savings
nodes.grounding_span: AI can pull the answer for me in seconds

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI efficiency
nodes.grounding_span: AI can make me feel satisfied when it provides a very specific answer that would have taken me a long time to find

nodes.label: Frustration with AI false confidence
nodes.grounding_span: I'd say I feel frustrated when the AI model provides me with information that is false but presents it as true

nodes.label: Preference for personal judgment in student feedback
nodes.grounding_span: I prefer to handle tasks that I am used to performing without AI

nodes.label: Openness to AI for new role learning
nodes.grounding_span: I ask AI questions about new terminology

nodes.label: Context-dependent interaction style — adapts between conversational and transactional based on task
nodes.grounding_span: I think my style of interaction depends on the type of conversation that I am having

nodes.label: Verification-oriented skepticism — double-checks AI when suspicion arises
nodes.grounding_span: I only double-check AI responses when I have a suspicion that the information is hard to find
